frameworks.name: computational head models
frameworks.one_line: MRI-derived volume conductor models predict tDCS current flow through individual brain anatomy

core_question: How do symmetric versus asymmetric DLPFC tDCS montages differ in current-flow focality and inter-individual variability across large-scale brain networks?
key_assumptions: MRI-based head models faithfully predict in-brain current flow during tDCS
Brain anatomy is the primary determinant of inter-individual response variability
Predicted current at targeted regions is mechanistically linked to behavioral and fMRI outcomes
manipulations: electrode montage geometry (symmetric vs asymmetric DLPFC)
key_findings: Asymmetric DLPFC montage produced more focal current flow than symmetric configuration
Asymmetric montage showed greater inter-individual variability in predicted current
Predicted current at targeted regions correlated with task-specific concurrent fMRI outcomes
interpretations: Montage choice trades focality against consistency of response across individuals
Individualized current-flow modeling links montage selection to functional brain-network engagement
